headline: Eli Lilly (LLY) After The China Verzenios Shift And The Valuation Debate
summary: Eli Lilly (LLY) is back in focus after handing Chinese partner Innovent Biologics full commercial control of breast cancer drug Verzenios, a shift that raises fresh questions about how the stock’s premium valuation stacks up. See our latest analysis for Eli Lilly. Verzenios is only one piece of a much bigger story for Eli Lilly. The stock gave back some ground in the latest session as the share price fell 1.14% to US$1,200.06, yet it is still showing firm momentum with a 30 day share price...

headline: Former Pfizer Building Is Crumbling—Just as the Stock Has
summary: Construction at  Pfizer’s  former headquarters in Manhattan was halted Tuesday because of crumbling floors and other structural issues.  The problems with the building’s renovation, which the company sold about eight years ago for conversion to apartments, are a metaphor of sorts for Pfizer stock, which began Tuesday with the highest yield in the  index, at more than 7%.  Pfizer stock, which is up 1.6% to $24.10 Tuesday, has been the worst performer among its large-cap U.S. peers during 2026 and over the past 10 years.

headline: Eli Lilly (LLY) Expands R And D Reach With Abbisko And China Drug Handover
summary: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has entered a new research collaboration with Abbisko Cayman Limited, focused on expanding its pipeline through external R&D partnerships. The company has transferred commercial rights for cancer drug Verzenios in China to Innovent Biologics, adjusting its approach in that market. Eli Lilly has become closely associated with obesity and diabetes treatments, but this latest update shows the company putting more emphasis on partnerships and regional focus. The Abbisko...
